news.news: GSK announced positive pivotal data for bepirovirsen, an investigational antisense oligonucleotide (ASO), achieving a 19% functional cure rate in chronic hepatitis B patients in the B-Well 1 and B-Well 2 Phase III trials. The results, published in the New England Journal of Medicine, showed a statistically significant improvement over placebo and support the drug's potential to redefine treatment for the disease.

The FDA approved Imfinzi (durvalumab) in combination with BCG for the treatment of BCG-naïve, high-risk non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC). This approval, based on the Phase III POTOMAC trial, marks the first new therapy in over 30 years for this setting, showing a 32% reduction in the risk of recurrence, progression, or death.

The CARES Phase III clinical programme for anselamimab, a monoclonal antibody targeting amyloid fibrils, failed to meet its primary endpoint in the overall light chain amyloidosis population. However, a prespecified subgroup analysis of patients with kappa light chain amyloidosis demonstrated a 62% improvement in survival and a 71% reduction in cardiovascular hospitalizations.

The U.S. FDA approved DECNUPAZ (pivekimab sunirine-pvzy), a CD123-targeting antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), for the treatment of adult patients with blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN). This is the first and only ADC approved for this ultra-rare blood cancer and marks AbbVie's first ADC approval for a blood cancer indication.

Pfizer and Innovent Biologics entered a global strategic collaboration to research and develop 12 early-stage and de novo cancer medicines. The portfolio includes antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and multi-specific antibodies, with Pfizer receiving exclusive global or outside-Greater China licenses for eight programs and co-developing four programs globally.

The National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China accepted new drug applications (NDAs) for toripalimab injection (subcutaneous) across 12 tumor indications. JS001sc is the first domestic anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody in a subcutaneous formulation to enter the marketing application stage, offering a more convenient administration method compared to intravenous options.
